name: Chinese Chicken Noodle Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 pkg. Top Ramen noodles, any flavor
1 chicken breast or 1/2 cup leftover chicken (turkey or pork)
1/2 cup chopped bok choy, Napa cabbage, or celery
recipeInstructions:
This soup adapts as ingredients are available in your refrigerator or pantry. It serves up quickly and is ready for the table in about 20 minutes.
1. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil. Add chicken broth and seasonings from the Top Ramen packet once boiling.
2. While the broth simmers, add the raw chicken breast (cut into small pieces), chopped vegetables, and raw egg.
3. The egg will cook into threads as it integrates with the soup.
4. Just before serving, incorporate noodles and peas, then sesame oil and scallions. Serve immediately to ensure vegetables remain slightly crunchy yet tender.
5. If the soup is too thick or you prefer more broth, add another can of chicken broth. Adjust seasoning with garlic and a dash of hot Chinese mustard as desired.
6. For strong-flavored vegetables like broccoli, cut them small and cook briefly to prevent overpowering flavors. The cooked noodles will absorb moisture from the broth, naturally thickening the soup.
